content.problemStatement:
Write a program to print all the **leaders** in the array. An element is a leader if it is strictly greater than all the elements to its right side. And the rightmost element is always a leader.

**Note on Order:** The output must maintain the original relative order of the leaders as they appear in the input array (from left to right).


Input Format

The first line contains NNN, the number of elements in an array.
The second line contains NNN integers, denoting the elements in the array.


Output Format

Print all the leader elements on a single line, separated by a space. The elements must be printed in the same left-to-right order they occur in the original array.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case, the first line contains an integer N, the number of elements in the array. The second line contains N space‑separated integers denoting the array elements.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line containing all leader elements of the given array in their original left‑to‑right order, separated by a single space.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Scanning from right, leaders are 2, then 5 (greater than 2), then 17 (greater than 5). Reversing gives 17 5 2.
Only the rightmost element 5 has no larger element to its right.
Every element is greater than all elements to its right, so all are leaders.

solution.approach: A leader is an element that is strictly greater than every element to its right. Scan the array from right to left while keeping the maximum seen so far. The rightmost element is always a leader. Whenever the current element is greater than the stored maximum, it becomes a new leader and we update the maximum. All found leaders are collected in reverse order; finally reverse the collection to restore the original left‑to‑right order. This runs in O(N) time per test case and O(N) auxiliary space for the result.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, where N is the size of the array.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) for storing the leaders (worst case when every element is a leader).

code.Python3:
import sys

def solve():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    out_lines = []
    for _ in range(T):
        n = int(next(it))
        arr = [int(next(it)) for _ in range(n)]
        leaders = []
        max_from_right = arr[-1]
        leaders.append(max_from_right)
        for x in reversed(arr[:-1]):
            if x > max_from_right:
                max_from_right = x
                leaders.append(x)
        out_lines.append(' '.join(map(str, reversed(leaders))))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out_lines))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    solve()
